1.title: Jacky Cupit born
1.text: American golfer Jacky Cupit, winner of four PGA Tour titles, was born on this day. He lost the 1963 U.S. Open in a three-way 18-hole playoff to Julius Boros and Arnold Palmer.

1.title: Jim Thorpe born
1.text: Jim Thorpe, a pioneering African American professional with three PGA Tour wins and 13 Champions Tour victories including the 2002 Tradition, was born on this day.

1.title: Tommy Bolt wins Tucson Open
1.text: Tommy Bolt shot 265 to win the Tucson Open by a single stroke over Chandler Harper. Bolt would go on to capture the 1958 U.S. Open.

1.title: Johnny Miller wins at Pebble Beach
1.text: Johnny Miller edged Payne Stewart by one stroke to win the AT&T Pebble Beach National Pro-Am on the Monterey Peninsula.

1.title: Jonathan Kaye wins FBR Open
1.text: Jonathan Kaye defeated Chris DiMarco by two shots to win the FBR Open (Phoenix) at TPC Scottsdale.

1.title: Kenny Perry wins FBR Open in playoff
1.text: Kenny Perry beat Charley Hoffman on the third hole of a sudden-death playoff to win the FBR Open in Phoenix.

1.title: Brooks Koepka's first PGA Tour win
1.text: Brooks Koepka closed with a 66, sparked by a 51-foot eagle putt, to win the WM Phoenix Open by one for his maiden PGA Tour title.

2.title: Stan Leonard born
2.text: Canadian golf great Stan Leonard, a three-time PGA Tour winner and Canadian Golf Hall of Fame member, was born on this day.

2.title: Pete Brown born
2.text: Pete Brown, the first African American to win a PGA Tour event when he captured the 1964 Waco Turner Open, was born on this day.

2.title: Jimmy Demaret wins Tucson Open
2.text: Jimmy Demaret shot 264 to win the Tucson Open by three strokes over Ben Hogan.

2.title: Fuzzy Zoeller wins rain-hit Pebble Beach
2.text: In a rain-shortened 54-hole event, Fuzzy Zoeller beat Payne Stewart by five to win the AT&T Pebble Beach National Pro-Am.

2.title: Mark O'Meara wins Pebble Beach in playoff
2.text: Mark O'Meara defeated Jeff Sluman on the first playoff hole to claim the AT&T Pebble Beach National Pro-Am, one of his record five wins there.

2.title: Webb Simpson wins WM Phoenix Open
2.text: Webb Simpson beat Tony Finau on the first playoff hole to win the WM Phoenix Open at TPC Scottsdale.

2.title: Rory McIlroy wins Pebble Beach Pro-Am
2.text: Rory McIlroy closed with a 66 to finish 21-under and beat Shane Lowry by two strokes, capturing the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am for his 27th PGA Tour title.

3.title: Tom Creavy born
3.text: Tom Creavy, who at age 20 won the 1931 PGA Championship by defeating Denny Shute in the final, was born on this day in Tuckahoe, New York.

3.title: Carol Mann born
3.text: World Golf Hall of Famer Carol Mann, winner of 38 LPGA Tour titles including the 1965 U.S. Women's Open, was born on this day.

3.title: Jimmy Demaret wins Tucson Open
3.text: Jimmy Demaret shot 268 to win the Tucson Open by four strokes over Herman Barron.

3.title: Retief Goosen born
3.text: South African Retief Goosen, a two-time U.S. Open champion (2001 and 2004) who reached World No. 1 in the rankings, was born on this day.

3.title: Mark O'Meara wins final Crosby Pro-Am
3.text: Mark O'Meara won the Bing Crosby National Pro-Am by one stroke in the last edition to carry the Crosby name before it became the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am.

3.title: Phil Mickelson wins WM Phoenix Open
3.text: Phil Mickelson, after an opening 60, cruised to a four-stroke win over Brandt Snedeker at the WM Phoenix Open.

3.title: Rickie Fowler wins WM Phoenix Open
3.text: Rickie Fowler held off a back-nine wobble to beat Branden Grace by two at the WM Phoenix Open.

4.title: Buck White born
4.text: Buck White, a three-time PGA Tour winner who in 1961 became the first player to shoot 63 in the PGA Seniors' Championship, was born on this day.

4.title: Byron Nelson born
4.text: Byron 'Lord Byron' Nelson, five-time major champion famed for his record 11 straight PGA Tour wins in 1945, was born on this day in Texas.

4.title: Lloyd Mangrum wins Tucson Open
4.text: Lloyd Mangrum shot 269 to win the Tucson Open by two strokes over a trio of challengers.

4.title: Arnold Palmer wins Palm Springs Classic
4.text: Arnold Palmer won the Palm Springs Golf Classic (later the Bob Hope) by three strokes over Jay Hebert and Gene Littler.

4.title: Arnold Palmer wins Bob Hope Classic
4.text: Arnold Palmer defeated Deane Beman in a playoff to win the Bob Hope Desert Classic.

4.title: Gary Woodland wins WM Phoenix Open
4.text: Gary Woodland beat Chez Reavie on the first playoff hole to win the WM Phoenix Open, a tune-up for his 2019 U.S. Open title.

5.title: Chandler Harper wins Tucson Open
5.text: Chandler Harper shot 267 to win the Tucson Open by two strokes over Sam Snead.

5.title: Jane Geddes born
5.text: American Jane Geddes, winner of 11 LPGA Tour titles including the 1986 U.S. Women's Open and 1987 LPGA Championship, was born on this day.

5.title: Jose Maria Olazabal born
5.text: Spaniard Jose Maria Olazabal, two-time Masters champion (1994 and 1999) and a Ryder Cup stalwart, was born on this day in the Basque Country.

5.title: Tom Nieporte wins Bob Hope Classic
5.text: Tom Nieporte won the Bob Hope Desert Classic by one stroke over Doug Sanders.

5.title: Mark Calcavecchia wins L.A. Open
5.text: Mark Calcavecchia shot 272 to edge Sandy Lyle by one stroke at the Nissan Los Angeles Open at Riviera, months before his Open Championship triumph.

5.title: J.B. Holmes wins FBR Open
5.text: Long-hitting rookie J.B. Holmes blew away the field, winning the FBR Open (Phoenix) by seven strokes.

5.title: Hideki Matsuyama wins WM Phoenix Open
5.text: Hideki Matsuyama beat Webb Simpson on the fourth playoff hole to defend his WM Phoenix Open title.

6.title: James Braid born
6.text: Scotland's James Braid, a five-time Open Championship winner and member of the 'Great Triumvirate' who later became a renowned course architect, was born on this day in Earlsferry, Fife.

6.title: Lloyd Mangrum wins Tucson Open
6.text: Lloyd Mangrum shot 263 to win the Tucson Open by five strokes over Al Smith.

6.title: Doug Sanders wins Bob Hope Classic
6.text: Doug Sanders defeated Arnold Palmer in a playoff to win the Bob Hope Desert Classic.

6.title: Tim Herron born
6.text: American Tim Herron, a four-time PGA Tour winner whose victories included the 1996 Honda Classic, was born on this day.

6.title: Richard Bland born
6.text: Englishman Richard Bland, who at 48 became the oldest first-time European Tour winner at the 2021 British Masters, was born on this day.

6.title: Ricky Barnes born
6.text: American Ricky Barnes, the 2002 U.S. Amateur champion and 2009 U.S. Open runner-up, was born on this day.

6.title: Mark Wilson wins Phoenix Open
6.text: Mark Wilson beat Jason Dufner in a playoff completed Monday morning after darkness halted play to win the Waste Management Phoenix Open.

7.title: Sebastian Miguel born
7.text: Spanish golf pioneer Sebastian Miguel, a three-time Spanish Open winner and frequent World Cup representative for Spain, was born on this day.

7.title: Arnold Palmer wins Palm Springs Classic
7.text: Arnold Palmer won the Palm Springs Desert Golf Classic, an early edition of the Bob Hope, by three strokes over Fred Hawkins.

7.title: Billy Casper wins Bob Hope Classic
7.text: Billy Casper edged Tommy Aaron and Arnold Palmer by one stroke to win the Bob Hope Desert Classic.

7.title: Jay Williamson born
7.text: American golfer Jay Williamson, a longtime PGA and Nationwide Tour player and St. Louis Sports Hall of Fame member, was born on this day.

7.title: Holly Clyburn born
7.text: Englishwoman Holly Clyburn, a Ladies European Tour player and 2012 Curtis Cup winner, was born on this day.

7.title: Steve Stricker wins Northern Trust Open
7.text: Steve Stricker shot 16-under 268 to hold off Luke Donald by two at Riviera, a win that returned him to World No. 2.

8.title: Bunky Henry born
8.text: PGA Tour winner Bunky Henry, who captured the 1969 National Airlines Open, was born on this day in Valdosta, Georgia.

8.title: Bruce Devlin wins Bob Hope Classic
8.text: Australian Bruce Devlin won the Bob Hope Desert Classic by four strokes over Larry Ziegler.

8.title: Raquel Carriedo born
8.text: Spaniard Raquel Carriedo, who topped the 2001 Ladies European Tour Order of Merit and became the first Spaniard in the Solheim Cup, was born on this day.

8.title: Johnny Miller wins Bob Hope Classic
8.text: Johnny Miller, in the midst of a dominant stretch, won the Bob Hope Desert Classic by three strokes over Rik Massengale.

8.title: Vijay Singh wins Pebble Beach Pro-Am
8.text: Vijay Singh beat Jeff Maggert by three strokes to win the AT&T Pebble Beach National Pro-Am during his ascent to World No. 1.

8.title: Nick Watney wins Buick Invitational
8.text: Nick Watney beat John Rollins by one stroke to win the Buick Invitational at Torrey Pines for his second PGA Tour title.

8.title: Jason Day wins Farmers Insurance Open
8.text: Jason Day prevailed in a four-man playoff at Torrey Pines to win the Farmers Insurance Open, a stepping stone to his major breakthrough later that year.

9.title: PGA Tour winner Bob Eastwood is born
9.text: American golfer Bob Eastwood was born on February 9, 1946. He won three PGA Tour titles in the mid-1980s, including the 1984 USF&G Classic and the 1985 Byron Nelson Classic.

9.title: Sandy Lyle, major champion, is born
9.text: Scottish golfer Sandy Lyle was born on February 9, 1958. He won the 1985 Open Championship and the 1988 Masters, becoming the first British player to win at Augusta.

9.title: Johnny Miller wins Bob Hope amid hot streak
9.text: On February 9, 1975, Johnny Miller won the Bob Hope Desert Classic in California, his third straight victory to open a blistering 1975 season.

9.title: Bob Tway wins first PGA Tour title at San Diego
9.text: On February 9, 1986, Bob Tway won the rain-shortened Andy Williams San Diego Open at Torrey Pines for his first PGA Tour win, months before he won the PGA Championship.

9.title: PGA Tour winner Michael Brennan is born
9.text: American golfer Michael Brennan was born on February 9, 2002. The former Wake Forest star won the 2025 Bank of Utah Championship for his first PGA Tour title on a sponsor's exemption.

9.title: Davis Love III wins at Pebble Beach
9.text: On February 9, 2003, Davis Love III won the AT&T Pebble Beach National Pro-Am, one of his multiple titles in the prestigious Monterey Peninsula event.

9.title: Thomas Detry wins first PGA Tour title in Phoenix
9.text: On February 9, 2025, Thomas Detry ran away with the WM Phoenix Open for his first PGA Tour win, becoming the first Belgian to win on the PGA Tour.

10.title: Greg Norman, world No. 1, is born
10.text: Australian golfer Greg Norman was born on February 10, 1955. He spent 331 weeks as world No. 1 and won the Open Championship in 1986 and 1993.

10.title: Andy Bean wins 1980 Hawaiian Open
10.text: On February 10, 1980, Andy Bean won the Hawaiian Open at Waialae Country Club in Honolulu, one of his eleven career PGA Tour titles.

10.title: Mark O'Meara wins 1985 Hawaiian Open
10.text: On February 10, 1985, Mark O'Meara captured the Hawaiian Open at Waialae Country Club in Honolulu for an early career PGA Tour victory.

10.title: Corey Pavin holes wedge to win Bob Hope
10.text: On February 10, 1991, Corey Pavin holed a wedge shot in the playoff to beat Mark O'Meara and win the Bob Hope Chrysler Classic.

10.title: LPGA star Lexi Thompson is born
10.text: American golfer Lexi Thompson was born on February 10, 1995. She became the youngest-ever LPGA Tour winner at 16 and captured the 2014 Kraft Nabisco Championship for her major title.

10.title: Olazabal wins Buick Invitational at Torrey Pines
10.text: On February 10, 2002, Jose Maria Olazabal closed strong to win the Buick Invitational at Torrey Pines, his first PGA Tour win since the 1999 Masters.

10.title: Steve Lowery wins Pebble Beach in playoff
10.text: On February 10, 2008, Steve Lowery beat Vijay Singh in a playoff to win the AT&T Pebble Beach National Pro-Am.

10.title: Brandt Snedeker wins 2013 Pebble Beach Pro-Am
10.text: On February 10, 2013, Brandt Snedeker won the AT&T Pebble Beach National Pro-Am on the Monterey Peninsula for one of his PGA Tour titles.

11.title: LPGA co-founder Helen Hicks is born
11.text: Helen Hicks, one of the 13 founders of the LPGA in 1950, was born on February 11, 1911. She won the 1937 Women's Western Open and the 1940 Titleholders.

11.title: PGA Tour winner Bert Greene is born
11.text: American golfer Bert Greene was born on February 11, 1944. He played the PGA Tour from 1967 to 1975 and won the 1973 Liggett Myers Open.

11.title: LPGA pro and teacher Cindy Miller is born
11.text: American golfer Cindy Miller was born on February 11, 1956. A two-time NCAA champion, she played the LPGA Tour and was named LPGA Teacher of the Year in 2010.

11.title: European Tour winner Dawie van der Walt is born
11.text: South African golfer Dawie van der Walt was born on February 11, 1983. He won twice on the European Tour in 2013, both events in South Africa.

11.title: LPGA winner Ryann O'Toole is born
11.text: American golfer Ryann O'Toole was born on February 11, 1987. A 2011 Solheim Cup pick, she won the 2021 Trust Golf Women's Scottish Open.

11.title: Ted Potter Jr. wins AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am
11.text: On February 11, 2018, Ted Potter Jr. closed at 17-under to win by three over a chase pack including Phil Mickelson and Dustin Johnson for his second PGA Tour title.

11.title: Nick Taylor wins WM Phoenix Open in playoff
11.text: On February 11, 2024, Canada's Nick Taylor birdied three of his last four holes, then beat Charley Hoffman in a playoff at TPC Scottsdale for his fourth PGA Tour title.

12.title: Ryder Cup player Ken Still is born
12.text: American golfer Ken Still was born on February 12, 1935. He won three PGA Tour titles and played on the 1969 U.S. Ryder Cup team.

12.title: U.S. Women's Amateur champion Cindy Hill is born
12.text: American golfer Cindy Hill was born on February 12, 1948. She won the 1974 U.S. Women's Amateur, played four Curtis Cups, and won twice on the LPGA Tour.

12.title: Irish Ryder Cup golfer Des Smyth is born
12.text: Irishman Des Smyth was born on February 12, 1953. He won eight European Tour titles across four decades and played on the 1979 and 1981 European Ryder Cup teams.

12.title: Arron Oberholser wins Pebble Beach Pro-Am
12.text: On February 12, 2006, Arron Oberholser won the AT&T Pebble Beach National Pro-Am for his only PGA Tour victory.

12.title: Mickelson wins fifth Pebble Beach Pro-Am title
12.text: After darkness suspended play Sunday, Phil Mickelson completed a closing 65 on Monday, February 12, 2007, beating Paul Casey by three for his record-tying fifth Pebble Beach Pro-Am title.

12.title: Mickelson outplays Tiger to win Pebble Beach
12.text: On February 12, 2012, Phil Mickelson shot a final-round 64 to overtake playing partner Tiger Woods and win the AT&T Pebble Beach National Pro-Am by two.

12.title: Jordan Spieth cruises to Pebble Beach title
12.text: On February 12, 2017, Jordan Spieth played bogey-free down the stretch to win the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am by four for his ninth PGA Tour win at age 23.

13.title: Patty Berg, LPGA founder, is born
13.text: Patty Berg, a founding member and first president of the LPGA, was born on February 13, 1918. She holds the all-time record of 15 women's major championships.

13.title: Two-time major runner-up Tommy Jacobs is born
13.text: American golfer Tommy Jacobs was born on February 13, 1935. He lost the 1964 U.S. Open to Ken Venturi and the 1966 Masters in a playoff to Jack Nicklaus.

13.title: PGA Tour player Hugh Royer III is born
13.text: American golfer Hugh Royer III was born on February 13, 1964. Son of 1970 Western Open champion Hugh Royer Jr., he earned his PGA Tour card via two 1995 Nike Tour wins.

13.title: Tiger Woods rallies to win Pebble Beach Pro-Am
13.text: On February 13, 2000, Tiger Woods birdied four of his last five holes to overtake Matt Gogel and win the AT&T Pebble Beach National Pro-Am, his sixth straight PGA Tour victory.

13.title: Bubba Watson wins Farmers Insurance Open
13.text: On February 13, 2011, Bubba Watson holed a long birdie putt on the 72nd hole to win the Farmers Insurance Open at Torrey Pines by one stroke.

13.title: Scheffler wins first PGA Tour title in Phoenix
13.text: On February 13, 2022, Scottie Scheffler claimed his first PGA Tour victory on the third playoff hole at the WM Phoenix Open, beating Patrick Cantlay at TPC Scottsdale.

14.title: Australian golf pioneer Norman Von Nida is born
14.text: Norman Von Nida, one of Australia's greatest golfers, was born on February 14, 1914. He topped the British PGA Order of Merit in 1947 with seven wins and mentored generations of Australians.

14.title: 1960 PGA champion Jay Hebert is born
14.text: American golfer Jay Hebert was born on February 14, 1923. He won the 1960 PGA Championship; he and brother Lionel are the only brothers to both win the PGA Championship.

14.title: Mickey Wright, 82-time LPGA winner, is born
14.text: Mickey Wright was born on February 14, 1935. She won 82 LPGA Tour events and 13 majors and is the only player to hold all four LPGA major titles at once.

14.title: Five-time PGA Tour winner Bob Murphy is born
14.text: American golfer Bob Murphy was born on February 14, 1943. He won five PGA Tour titles and 11 Senior PGA Tour events and became a longtime TV golf commentator.

14.title: Champions Tour winner Bruce Summerhays is born
14.text: American golfer Bruce Summerhays was born on February 14, 1944. He won three times on the Champions Tour, including the 1998 State Farm Senior Classic.

14.title: PGA Tour winner Barry Jaeckel is born
14.text: American golfer Barry Jaeckel was born on February 14, 1949. He won the 1978 Tallahassee Open in a playoff over Bruce Lietzke for his lone PGA Tour title.

14.title: Dustin Johnson repeats at Pebble Beach Pro-Am
14.text: On February 14, 2010, Dustin Johnson birdied the 18th on Sunday to win the AT&T Pebble Beach National Pro-Am for the second straight year.

15.title: Three-time PGA Tour winner Cameron Beckman is born
15.text: American golfer Cameron Beckman was born on February 15, 1970. He won three PGA Tour events, including the 2010 Mayakoba Golf Classic.

15.title: J.C. Snead wins 1976 San Diego Open
15.text: On February 15, 1976, J.C. Snead, nephew of Sam Snead, won the Andy Williams-San Diego Open Invitational for one of his eight PGA Tour victories.

15.title: Four-time PGA Tour winner Kevin Kisner is born
15.text: American golfer Kevin Kisner was born on February 15, 1984. He won four PGA Tour titles, including the 2019 WGC-Dell Technologies Match Play, and played two Presidents Cups.

15.title: George Burns wins San Diego Open with record 266
15.text: On February 15, 1987, George Burns won the San Diego Open with a tournament-record 266 (-22), a mark Tiger Woods later matched in 1999.

15.title: John Huston wins 1998 Hawaiian Open
15.text: On February 15, 1998, John Huston won the United Airlines Hawaiian Open at Waialae Country Club in Honolulu, one of his seven PGA Tour titles.

15.title: John Daly ends drought at Buick Invitational
15.text: On February 15, 2004, John Daly won the Buick Invitational at Torrey Pines in a playoff over Chris Riley and Luke Donald, ending a nearly nine-year PGA Tour victory drought.

15.title: Brandt Snedeker wins 2015 Pebble Beach Pro-Am
15.text: On February 15, 2015, Brandt Snedeker won the AT&T Pebble Beach National Pro-Am for the second time for his seventh PGA Tour victory.

16.title: LPGA co-founder Marlene Hagge born
16.text: Marlene Hagge (née Bauer), born February 16, 1934, was the youngest of the 13 LPGA founders in 1950. She won 26 LPGA titles including the 1956 LPGA Championship and was the last surviving founder when she died in 2023.

16.title: Japanese golf great Hideyo Sugimoto born
16.text: Hideyo Sugimoto, a Japanese professional golfer born February 16, 1938, won multiple times in Japan and captured the Taiwan Open, becoming one of the top players of Japan's pre-tour era in the 1960s and 1970s.

16.title: Japan Tour winner Saburo Fujiki born
16.text: Saburo Fujiki, born February 16, 1955, in Mie, Japan, won 14 events on the Japan Golf Tour during his career.

16.title: Hawaiian Open champion John Morse born
16.text: John Paul Morse, born February 16, 1958, won the 1995 Hawaiian Open for his lone PGA Tour title and also captured the 1990 Australian Open before playing the PGA Tour Champions.

16.title: Two-time major winner Stacy Lewis born
16.text: Stacy Lewis, born February 16, 1985, in Toledo, Ohio, won two LPGA majors (2011 Kraft Nabisco, 2013 Women's British Open), reached World No. 1, and captained the U.S. Solheim Cup team.

16.title: PING founder Karsten Solheim dies at 88
16.text: Karsten Solheim, the engineer who founded PING and revolutionized putter and iron design, died February 16, 2000, in Phoenix at age 88. The Solheim Cup is named in his honor.

16.title: Aberg wins Genesis Invitational at Torrey Pines
16.text: Ludvig Aberg birdied the 72nd hole to win the 2025 Genesis Invitational by one stroke over Maverick McNealy on February 16, 2025. The event was moved from Riviera to Torrey Pines due to the Los Angeles wildfires.

16.title: Morikawa wins AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am
16.text: Collin Morikawa birdied three of his final four holes, including the par-5 18th, to win the 2026 AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am by one shot over Min Woo Lee on February 16, 2026, his seventh PGA Tour title.

17.title: Long-drive legend Mike Austin born
17.text: Michael Hoke Austin, born February 17, 1910, holds the Guinness record for the longest drive in a professional tournament, 515 yards at the 1974 U.S. National Seniors Open in Las Vegas, at age 64.

17.title: Danish golfer Thomas Norret born
17.text: Thomas Norret, born February 17, 1974, is a Danish professional golfer who won the 2002 Volvo Finnish Open on the Challenge Tour and reached the European Tour in 2010.

17.title: PGA Tour pro Martin Flores born
17.text: Martin Flores, born February 17, 1982, in Fort Worth, Texas, played several seasons on the PGA Tour and won the 2016 Lincoln Land Championship on the Web.com (Korn Ferry) Tour.

17.title: PGA Tour player Jon Curran born
17.text: Jon Curran, born February 17, 1987, in Hopkinton, Massachusetts, played college golf at Vanderbilt and reached the PGA Tour, with runner-up finishes at the 2015 Puerto Rico Open and 2016 Memorial.

17.title: Spanish DP World Tour winner Nacho Elvira born
17.text: Spaniard Ignacio 'Nacho' Elvira, born February 17, 1987, in Madrid, has won twice on the European/DP World Tour, including the 2024 Soudal Open.

17.title: Mickelson wins first Riviera title at Northern Trust
17.text: Phil Mickelson closed out the 2008 Northern Trust Open at Riviera on February 17, 2008, beating Jeff Quinney by two shots for his 33rd PGA Tour win and his long-awaited first victory at Riviera Country Club.

18.title: Open champion Sandy Herd dies at 75
18.text: Scottish professional Alexander 'Sandy' Herd, winner of the 1902 Open Championship at Hoylake and the first Open champ to use the Haskell rubber-cored ball, died February 18, 1944, at age 75.

18.title: LPGA Hall of Famer Judy Rankin born
18.text: Judy Rankin, born February 18, 1945, in St. Louis, won 26 LPGA titles, topped the money list in 1976 and 1977, and became the first LPGA player to earn over $100,000 in a season before a long broadcasting career.

18.title: Ryder Cup captain Thomas Bjorn born
18.text: Thomas Bjorn, born February 18, 1971, in Silkeborg, Denmark, became the first Danish Ryder Cup player and captained Europe to victory at the 2018 Ryder Cup, winning 15 European Tour titles.

18.title: Bob Dickson wins San Diego Open at Torrey Pines
18.text: Bob Dickson won the Andy Williams-San Diego Open Invitational (today's Farmers Insurance Open) at Torrey Pines on February 18, 1973, finishing 278 (-10) to win by one stroke.

18.title: Dan Forsman wins at Torrey Pines
18.text: Dan Forsman captured the Shearson Lehman Hutton Open (today's Farmers Insurance Open) at Torrey Pines on February 18, 1990, shooting 275 (-13) to win by two strokes over Tommy Armour III.

18.title: Howell beats Mickelson in playoff at Riviera
18.text: Charles Howell III closed with a 65 and defeated Phil Mickelson on the third hole of sudden death to win the 2007 Nissan Open at Riviera Country Club on February 18, 2007.

18.title: Bubba Watson wins third Genesis Open at Riviera
18.text: Bubba Watson shot 272 (-12) to win the 2018 Genesis Open by two strokes over Kevin Na and Tony Finau on February 18, claiming his third title at Riviera and 10th PGA Tour win.

18.title: Matsuyama's record 62 wins Genesis Invitational
18.text: Hideki Matsuyama fired a final-round 62 from six shots back to win the 2024 Genesis Invitational at Riviera at 267 (-17) on February 18, the lowest closing round ever by a winner there, by three strokes.

19.title: Augusta National's first pro Ed Dudley born
19.text: Ed Dudley, born February 19, 1901, in Brunswick, Georgia, won 15 PGA Tour events, served as Augusta National's first head professional (1932-1957), and was PGA of America president from 1942-1948.

19.title: Course design pioneer Alice Dye born
19.text: Alice Dye, born February 19, 1927, in Indianapolis, was a champion amateur and pioneering course architect who conceived the island green 17th at TPC Sawgrass and was the first woman president of the ASGCA.

19.title: PGA Tour winner Bob Menne born
19.text: Bob Menne, born February 19, 1942, in Gardner, Massachusetts, won the 1974 Kemper Open in a playoff over Jerry Heard during his PGA Tour career.

19.title: Australian Richard Green born
19.text: Richard Green, born February 19, 1971, in Australia, won three European Tour titles, beginning with the 1997 Dubai Desert Classic where he beat Greg Norman and Ian Woosnam in a playoff.

19.title: Sabbatini rallies to win Nissan Open
19.text: Rory Sabbatini began the final round nine shots back, then surged to win the 2006 Nissan Open at Riviera at 271 (-13) on February 19, one stroke ahead of Adam Scott.

19.title: Dustin Johnson rises to World No. 1 with Genesis win
19.text: Dustin Johnson won the 2017 Genesis Open at Riviera by five strokes on February 19, a victory that lifted him to World No. 1 for the first time and ended Jason Day's 47-week reign.

19.title: Jon Rahm wins Genesis, returns to World No. 1
19.text: Jon Rahm shot 267 (-17) to win the 2023 Genesis Invitational at Riviera by two strokes over Max Homa on February 19, his third win of the year, returning him to World No. 1.

20.title: Three-time PGA Tour winner Jeff Maggert born
20.text: Jeff Maggert, born February 20, 1964, in Columbia, Missouri, won three PGA Tour titles and played on three U.S. Ryder Cup teams. He is the only golfer to record multiple double eagles in major championships.

20.title: Tom Purtzer wins LA Open for first Tour title
20.text: Tom Purtzer captured the Glen Campbell Los Angeles Open at Riviera on February 20, 1977, beating Lanny Wadkins by one stroke after a clutch par save on the 72nd hole for the first of his PGA Tour victories.

20.title: Kirk Triplett breaks through at Nissan Open
20.text: Kirk Triplett won the 2000 Nissan Open at Riviera by one shot over Jesper Parnevik on February 20, claiming his first PGA Tour title at age 37 after a decade on tour.

20.title: Future world No. 1 Jeeno Thitikul born
20.text: Atthaya 'Jeeno' Thitikul, born February 20, 2003, in Ratchaburi, Thailand, became Rolex Women's World No. 1, a multiple LPGA Tour winner and LPGA Rookie of the Year.

20.title: Aaron Baddeley wins at Riviera
20.text: Aaron Baddeley closed with a 69 to beat Vijay Singh by two at the 2011 Northern Trust Open at Riviera on February 20, ending a four-year victory drought on the PGA Tour.

20.title: Joaquin Niemann goes wire-to-wire at Genesis
20.text: Joaquin Niemann won the 2022 Genesis Invitational at Riviera wire-to-wire on February 20, the first to do so since Charlie Sifford in 1969, beating Collin Morikawa and Cameron Young by two.

21.title: Ryder Cup veteran Maurice Bembridge born
21.text: English golfer Maurice Bembridge, born February 21, 1945, played in four Ryder Cups and won six European Tour events. His final-round 64 at the 1974 Masters long stood as a joint course record.

21.title: New Zealand's Greg Turner born
21.text: Greg Turner, born February 21, 1963, in Dunedin, New Zealand, was a four-time European Tour winner and two-time New Zealand Open champion who played on the winning 1998 International Presidents Cup team.

21.title: Swedish Ryder Cupper Pierre Fulke born
21.text: Pierre Fulke, born February 21, 1971, in Nykoping, Sweden, was a three-time European Tour winner who played on Europe's victorious 2002 Ryder Cup team at The Belfry.

21.title: Ernie Els wins Nissan Open at Riviera
21.text: Ernie Els closed with a 68 to win the 1999 Nissan Open at Riviera Country Club at 14-under par on February 21, adding a West Coast title during his prime years.

21.title: Bubba Watson rallies to win at Riviera again
21.text: Bubba Watson birdied two of his last three holes to overtake Jason Kokrak and hold off Adam Scott by one at the 2016 Northern Trust Open on February 21, his second Riviera title in three years.

21.title: Hometown hero Max Homa wins Genesis Invitational
21.text: Los Angeles native Max Homa beat Tony Finau in a playoff to win the 2021 Genesis Invitational at Riviera on February 21, receiving the trophy from host Tiger Woods for his second PGA Tour title.

22.title: 1973 Masters champion Tommy Aaron born
22.text: Tommy Aaron, born February 22, 1937, in Gainesville, Georgia, won the 1973 Masters and played on two U.S. Ryder Cup teams.

22.title: Hall of Famer Vijay Singh born
22.text: Vijay Singh, born February 22, 1963, in Lautoka, Fiji, is a three-time major champion (1998 and 2004 PGA Championships, 2000 Masters) and former World No. 1 with 34 PGA Tour wins.

22.title: Mike Weir defends Nissan Open title in playoff
22.text: Mike Weir erased a large final-round deficit and beat Charles Howell III on the second playoff hole to win the 2004 Nissan Open at Riviera on February 22, the first to win back-to-back there since Corey Pavin.

22.title: Phil Mickelson defends title at Riviera
22.text: Phil Mickelson made late birdies on the 16th and 17th to edge Steve Stricker by one and defend his Northern Trust Open title at Riviera on February 22, 2009, his 35th PGA Tour win.

22.title: Lydia Ko becomes youngest Women's Aussie Open champ
22.text: At age 17, Lydia Ko won the ISPS Handa Women's Australian Open by two over Amy Yang at Royal Melbourne on February 22, 2015, becoming the youngest winner of the event for her sixth LPGA Tour title.

22.title: James Hahn wins Northern Trust Open in playoff
22.text: James Hahn drained a long birdie putt on the third playoff hole to beat Dustin Johnson and Paul Casey at the 2015 Northern Trust Open at Riviera on February 22, earning his first PGA Tour title.

23.title: Bobby Mitchell born, beat Nicklaus in playoff
23.text: American golfer Bobby Mitchell was born this day. He won twice on the PGA Tour, including the 1972 Tournament of Champions where he defeated Jack Nicklaus in a playoff.

23.title: LPGA player Cindy Figg-Currier born
23.text: American professional golfer Cindy Figg-Currier, a longtime LPGA Tour competitor, was born on this date.

23.title: Steve Stricker born in Edgerton, Wisconsin
23.text: Twelve-time PGA Tour winner Steve Stricker was born this day. A future world No. 2 and U.S. Ryder Cup captain, he won the 2001 WGC-Accenture Match Play Championship.

23.title: Michael Campbell born, future U.S. Open champ
23.text: New Zealand golfer Michael Campbell was born this day. He won the 2005 U.S. Open, holding off Tiger Woods, and the HSBC World Match Play in the same year.

23.title: Swedish star Carin Koch born
23.text: Carin Koch, a Swedish professional who won on the LPGA Tour and later captained Europe's 2015 Solheim Cup team, was born on this date.

23.title: Mike Weir wins Nissan Open at Riviera
23.text: Mike Weir rallied from seven back to catch Charles Howell III and won the Nissan Open in a playoff at Riviera, a key win in his banner 2003 season that included the Masters.

24.title: Victorian-era golfer Henry Lamb born
24.text: Henry Lamb, a British amateur golfer of the Victorian era who competed in early Open Championships, was born this day in India. He died in 1893.

24.title: Jim Ferrier born, first Aussie major winner
24.text: Australian-born Jim Ferrier was born this day. He won the 1947 PGA Championship, becoming the first Australian to capture a men's major championship, and won 18 PGA Tour titles.

24.title: Zach Johnson born in Iowa City
24.text: Two-time major champion Zach Johnson was born this day. He won the 2007 Masters and the 2015 Open Championship, and captained the U.S. at the 2023 Ryder Cup.

24.title: PGA Tour player Ben Kohles born
24.text: American professional golfer Ben Kohles, a Korn Ferry Tour and PGA Tour competitor, was born this day in Dallas.

24.title: South African J.C. Ritchie born
24.text: J.C. Ritchie, a South African professional golfer who has won on the Sunshine Tour, was born on this date.

25.title: PGA Tour winner Henry Ransom born
25.text: American golfer Henry Ransom, a multiple PGA Tour winner from Houston, Texas, was born on this date.

25.title: 'Champagne' Tony Lema born in Oakland
25.text: Tony Lema was born this day. Nicknamed 'Champagne Tony,' he won the 1964 Open Championship at St Andrews by five shots over Jack Nicklaus before dying in a 1966 plane crash.

25.title: Spanish golfer Juan Quiros born
25.text: Juan Quiros, a Spanish professional golfer who later found success on the European Senior Tour, was born this day in La Linea de la Concepcion.

25.title: Hideki Matsuyama born in Japan
25.text: Hideki Matsuyama was born this day. He became the first Japanese man to win a major at the 2021 Masters and won back-to-back WM Phoenix Opens in 2016 and 2017.

25.title: Justin Thomas wins Honda Classic in playoff
25.text: Justin Thomas birdied the first playoff hole to beat Luke List at PGA National, claiming the 2018 Honda Classic for his eighth PGA Tour title.

26.title: Canadian pro Harold Eidsvig born
26.text: Harold Eidsvig, a Canadian professional golfer from the Winnipeg area, was born on this date.

26.title: Amateur great Steve Melnyk born
26.text: Steve Melnyk was born this day. He won the 1969 U.S. Amateur and the 1971 British Amateur before turning pro and later becoming a noted golf broadcaster.

26.title: PGA Tour winner Jim Ahern born
26.text: American golfer Jim Ahern, a PGA Tour player from Duluth, Minnesota, was born on this date.

26.title: Taiwan's Lu Wen-teh born
26.text: Lu Wen-teh, a Taiwanese professional golfer who competed widely on the Asian and Japan tours, was born on this date.

26.title: Australian LPGA winner Katherine Kirk born
26.text: Katherine Kirk, an Australian professional who has won multiple LPGA Tour titles, was born this day in Brisbane.

26.title: Rickie Fowler wins the Honda Classic
26.text: Rickie Fowler closed with a 71 for a four-shot victory at PGA National, ending a long winless stretch to capture the 2017 Honda Classic.

27.title: Gene Sarazen born, future Grand Slam winner
27.text: Gene Sarazen was born this day. Winner of seven majors, he became the first golfer to complete the career Grand Slam and is credited with popularizing the modern sand wedge.

27.title: PGA Tour winner Ryan Armour born
27.text: American golfer Ryan Armour, who won the 2017 Sanderson Farms Championship for his first PGA Tour title, was born this day in Akron, Ohio.

27.title: Colombian golfer Mariajo Uribe born
27.text: Mariajo Uribe, a Colombian professional who won the 2007 U.S. Women's Amateur and played on the LPGA Tour, was born on this date.

27.title: Abraham Ancer born in Texas
27.text: Abraham Ancer, a Mexican professional golfer and 2021 Presidents Cup International team member, was born this day in McAllen, Texas.

27.title: LPGA winner Jessica Korda born
27.text: Jessica Korda, a six-time LPGA Tour winner and elder sister of world No. 1 Nelly Korda, was born this day in Bradenton, Florida.

27.title: Sepp Straka wins the Honda Classic
27.text: Sepp Straka rallied from five back with a final-round 66 to edge Shane Lowry by one, becoming the first Austrian to win on the PGA Tour at the 2022 Honda Classic.

28.title: Early U.S. golf figure Basil Spalding de Garmendia born
28.text: Basil Spalding de Garmendia, an American sportsman active in the formative years of U.S. golf and court tennis, was born on this date in Baltimore.

28.title: Peter Alliss, 'Voice of Golf,' born
28.text: Peter Alliss was born this day. A Ryder Cup player who won numerous European events, he became golf's most beloved broadcaster, known as the 'Voice of Golf.'

28.title: PGA Tour player Rex Baxter born
28.text: Rex Baxter, an American golfer who won the 1957 U.S. Junior Amateur and played the PGA Tour, was born this day in Amarillo, Texas.

28.title: Irish golfer Hugh Jackson born
28.text: Hugh Jackson, a Northern Irish professional golfer who competed on the European circuit, was born this day in Newtownards. He died in 2015.

28.title: Australian pro Jeff Woodland born
28.text: Jeff Woodland, an Australian professional golfer who won on the PGA Tour of Australasia, was born on this date.

28.title: Japan's Kiyoshi Miyazato born
28.text: Kiyoshi Miyazato, a Japanese professional golfer and multiple Japan Golf Tour winner, was born this day in Okinawa.

29.title: Tiger Woods defends WGC Match Play title
29.text: On a rare leap day, Tiger Woods beat Davis Love III in the final at La Costa to defend his WGC-Accenture Match Play Championship crown.

29.title: Heath Slocum wins Chrysler Classic of Tucson
29.text: On this leap day, Heath Slocum captured the Chrysler Classic of Tucson for his first PGA Tour victory, the same Sunday Tiger Woods won the WGC Match Play.

29.title: Annika Sorenstam wins third ANZ Ladies Masters
29.text: On this leap day, Annika Sorenstam claimed her third ANZ Ladies Masters title in Australia, one of the few professional golf events ever decided on February 29.

29.title: Golf's rarest calendar date
29.text: February 29 falls only once every four years, making it the scarcest date in golf history; in most leap years tour play falls on the PGA Tour's West Coast Swing and the season's early World Golf Championships.

29.title: Leap-day action on the PGA Tour
29.text: On this leap day the PGA Tour was in the middle of its Florida and West Coast stretch, with the game's elite jockeying for position early in the season before the year's first major at Augusta.
